findings.evidence: The declarations at lines 60, 79, 87, 96, and 105 use `GridPoint.I/JNum`, `GridState.J`, `pointSet`, and `x.toPerm`, while `GradingInteger.lean` defines `maslovOℤ`/`maslovXℤ` only later at lines 67 and 80.
findings.fix: Move these `GridState` lemmas to `JFunction.lean` or to a new earlier file importing `JFunction`; keep only `maslovOℤ_eq_card` and `maslovXℤ_eq_card` at the integer-grading level.
findings.issue: `I_pointSet_eq`, `JNum_pointSet_eq`, `J_pointSet_eq`, `I_self_pointSet_eq`, and `card_filter_ascent_add_card_filter_descent` are generic grid-state/J-function facts but live in a file importing `Grid.GradingInteger`.

summary: The column-index API is useful, but the file places broadly reusable `GridState`/`GridPoint.J` lemmas behind the later integer-grading module. Split the earlier `J`-function material from the Maslov integer consequences so downstream files can import the right level.
